tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hi there! I wanted to let you know that I'd prefer to receive concise updates about Newham council activities rather than long detailed reports. Could you remember that for me?

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "User prefers to receive concise updates about Newham council activities rather than long detailed reports."}

messages.content: {"memory": {"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "User prefers to receive concise updates about Newham council activities rather than long detailed reports."}}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content: Got it! I've noted that you'd prefer concise updates about Newham council activities. I'll keep that in mind for any future communications.
Hi, could you show me what civic preferences you've stored so far? I'd like to verify what you remember about my updates.
{"memories": [{"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "User prefers to receive concise updates about Newham council activities rather than long detailed reports."}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
Here's the civic preference I have stored for you:

- **Communication preference:** You prefer to receive concise updates about Newham council activities rather than long detailed reports.

Is there anything else you'd like me to add or adjust?
Could you show me all the memories you've stored for me, not just the civic ones? I'd like to see everything you have saved so far.

I don't have any other memories stored beyond the one I just showed you. The only memory currently saved is:

- **Civic preference:** You prefer concise updates about Newham council activities rather than long detailed reports.

Is there something specific you'd like me to remember for you?
Actually, on second thought, I'd like to remove that civic preference you stored. I've changed my mind and would rather receive the detailed reports after all. Could you delete that memory for me?
Done! I've deleted that civic preference memory. You'll now receive detailed reports about Newham council activities. Let me know if you'd like me to store any other preferences for you.

structured_turns.assistant_message: Got it! I've noted that you'd prefer concise updates about Newham council activities. I'll keep that in mind for any future communications.
